title: Understanding Credit Scores
content:
Understanding Credit Scores: Complete Guide

Your credit score is a three-digit number that represents your creditworthiness. Scores range from 300 to 850.

Credit Score Ranges

  • Excellent: 800-850
  • Very Good: 740-799
  • Good: 670-739
  • Fair: 580-669
  • Poor: 300-579

5 Factors Affecting Your FICO Score

  • Payment History (35%): Your track record of on-time payments
  • Amounts Owed (30%): Your credit utilization ratio
  • Length of Credit History (15%): How long accounts have been open
  • Credit Mix (10%): Variety of credit types
  • New Credit (10%): Recent credit inquiries and new accounts

seo.meta_title: Understanding Credit Scores: Complete Guide | Credlocity

title: Collection Removal Services
content:
Remove Collection Accounts with Credlocity

Our team of experts specializes in removing collection accounts from your credit report using proven legal strategies.

Our Expertise

We leverage the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act (FDCPA) and advanced dispute methods to remove collections.

Our Process

  1. Comprehensive credit report review
  2. Identify all collection accounts
  3. Analyze for inaccuracies and FDCPA violations
  4. Develop customized dispute strategy
  5. Execute disputes with bureaus and collectors
  6. Negotiate pay-for-delete when appropriate
  7. Monitor and follow up
  8. Document all results

Common FDCPA Violations We Find

  • Calling outside permitted hours
  • Harassment or abuse
  • False statements about debt
  • Threatening illegal actions
  • Failure to provide validation

title: Frequently Asked Questions
content:
Frequently Asked Questions

About Our Services

What is credit repair?

Credit repair is the process of identifying and disputing inaccurate, incomplete, or unverifiable information on your credit reports to improve your credit score.

How long does credit repair take?

Most clients see results within 30-90 days, though complex cases may take longer. Results vary based on individual circumstances.

Is credit repair legal?

Yes, credit repair is 100% legal. The Fair Credit Reporting Act (FCRA) gives you the right to dispute inaccurate information on your credit reports.

About Pricing

Do you charge first work fees?

No. Unlike many competitors, we charge $0 in first work fees. You only pay your monthly plan fee after your free trial.

What's included in the 30-day free trial?

Full access to all services: credit analysis, dispute initiation, expert consultations, credit monitoring, and educational resources.

About Results

Can you guarantee specific results?

We cannot guarantee specific outcomes as results depend on individual circumstances and credit bureau decisions. However, we offer a 100% money-back guarantee if you're not satisfied.

What types of items can you remove?

We can dispute and potentially remove: late payments, collections, charge-offs, bankruptcies (if reported incorrectly), inquiries, and fraudulent accounts.
